basmala: In the Name of God, the Infinitely Merciful, the Most Merciful.
intro: Al-Kāfirūn — “The Disbelievers” — is among the early Meccan sūras, revealed shortly after al-Māʿūn (sūra 107). It takes its name from the word with which the Prophet is told to address, throughout the text, those who reject faith. Tradition recounts that it was revealed in answer to a proposal from the leaders of Quraysh: “Muḥammad, come, let us worship what you worship, and you shall worship what we worship; we shall come together in this matter. If what you worship is better, we shall take a share of it; if what we worship is better, you shall take a share of it.” The Prophet replied: “God forbid that I associate anything with Him.” Then the sūra was revealed. Six very brief verses that state, without ambiguity, the separation of worships. With sūra 112 al-Ikhlāṣ — the full affirmation of the True — al-Kāfirūn, the full denial of the false, forms the Muqashqishatān, “the two cures”.

versets.commentaire: al-kāfirūn (الكَافِرُون): those who cover. The root k-f-r first means “to veil, to cover” — the word names the farmer who covers the seed under earth; it came to designate the one who covers the received truth, who denies it. At the time of revelation, the address aims at the leaders of Quraysh who had proposed a compromise, but its reach extends to every denier in every age.

mots.def: vocative particle: O (used to call out).
kāfir: the one who covers, who veils the received truth, who denies it; plural al-kāfirūn. Root k-f-r: to cover.

versets.trad: I do not worship what you worship,
versets.commentaire: The verb ʿabada (عَبَدَ) means to serve, to worship; the root carries the sense of humility, of submission — a ʿabd (عَبْد) is at once servant and worshipper. The declaration is plain: there is no community between the worship of the One God and that of the idols. No share, no pact.

mots.def: negative particle: not; negates the imperfect.
ʿabada: to worship, to serve in submission; imperfect, 1st pers. sing.
ʿabada: to worship; imperfect plural, 2nd pers.

versets.trad: nor do you worship what I worship.
versets.commentaire: The symmetry is exact: neither you, nor I. The use of the participle ʿābidūn (عَابِدُون), “worshippers”, instead of a simple verb, underlines a lasting attitude, an identity — not an isolated act but the steady orientation of the heart. And that orientation does not mingle.

mots.def: active participle of ʿabada: worshipper, one who worships; plural names a lasting posture.
ʿabada: to worship; imperfect, 1st pers. sing.

versets.trad: And I shall not worship what you have worshipped,
versets.commentaire: The form shifts to the past: mā ʿabadtum (مَا عَبَدتُّمْ), “what you have worshipped”. The sūra runs across the whole of time. Some commentators hear the repetition as enclosing the separation in every dimension of time: not now, not in the past, not in the future. Others see in it simply a strengthening by redundancy — a definitive closing of the door of compromise.

mots.def: active participle of ʿabada: worshipper; indefinite singular.
ʿabada: to worship; perfect, 2nd pers. masc. plur.: you have worshipped.

versets.trad: nor will you worship what I worship.
versets.commentaire: The sūra repeats v. 3 exactly, like a refrain. For some commentators this parallel says continuity through time: not yesterday, not tomorrow, not now nor later, the two orientations do not mingle. It is a door drawn shut — not in hostility, but out of inner exigency.

versets.trad: Unto you your religion, and unto me my religion.”
versets.commentaire: The verse closes the sūra with a formula that has become famous. Dīn (دِين) — “religion”, but also “way”, “due order”, “judgment” — is said twice, each side mirroring the other. Many have heard a word of peace: not aggression, but separation. Others read in it a warning: each shall have the fruit of his way. The Quran says elsewhere: “Be gentle with the disbelievers; grant them respite for a while” (86:17). The steadfastness of faith does not require violence toward the other, but the irreducibility of one's own.

mots.def: preposition li- + suffix pronoun 2nd pers. masc. plur.: to you, for you.
dīn: religion, way, due order, judgment; with the plural suffix pronoun: your religion.
preposition li- + suffix pronoun 1st pers. sing.: to me, for me.
dīn: religion, way; the possessive suffix -ī (“my religion”) is here elided in pronunciation, in conformity with the verse's diction.
